answer:
A. Senior engineers are pattern-matched to distrust B's clickbait formula — second-person fearmongering ("Hiding in Your...") plus a suspiciously precise dollar figure reads as content marketing. A is a plain postmortem-style claim about *your own* system, which is exactly the genre skeptical engineers actually read and share (HN-style "how we did X" posts). It promises concrete details and invites verification rather than presuming the reader has the same bug.

If 80% sounds too good to be true, ground it: "How We Cut Our AWS Bill 80% by Fixing One Autoscaling Setting."
